text.title: Spatial Asset Pipeline Rules
text.summary: Asset-budget rules, format-choice enforcement, agentic workflow guidance, and known failure modes for spatial design pipelines.

section.title: Asset Budget Rules

rule.statement: Set polygon count and texture size budgets at concept stage, before any artist work begins. Enforce budgets in CI via a lint script.
rule.rationale: Performance budgets only become real constraints inside the target engine. Concept-tool exports (ShapesXR, Gravity Sketch) do not respect engine budgets; artists who build without targets spend days optimizing under deadline pressure.

rule.statement: Declare unit (meters), axis convention (Y-up vs. Z-up), and handedness in every export script header. Reject assets that omit these declarations.
rule.rationale: A missing axis-swap flag produces subtly broken assets that look correct in one tool and appear flipped or at wrong scale in the target engine. LLMs omit these consistently — enforce at the pipeline level.

section.reference: CI script: fail build if glTF asset exceeds polygon or size budget

rule.statement: Maintain one shared asset repository; tool-specific copies (Figma, ShapesXR, Unity, Unreal) must always derive from the shared repo, never fork from each other.
rule.rationale: Tool-specific copies drift within a single sprint. A Gravity Sketch export edited in Blender and re-imported to Unity without touching the shared repo creates a fork that silently diverges.

rule.statement: Test in the target headset by week 2 of any project. Concept-tool fidelity hides 80% of comfort issues (interpupillary distance, locomotion sickness, focus distance).
rule.rationale: Comfort issues surface only in hardware. Simulator and desktop previews mask vergence-accommodation conflict, edge distortion, and locomotion nausea.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Agents can: (a) recommend the right tool per project phase given device and team constraints, (b) automate format conversions and asset-budget linting, (c) generate handoff documentation. Tool choice itself remains human — the team's existing skill stack dominates the decision.
Useful subagents: faion-ux-researcher-agent (assembles tool comparison matrix from vendor docs), faion-sdd-executor-agent (implements export/conversion scripts and CI hooks once tooling is selected).

antipattern.description: Recommending Three.js for a Vision Pro project or Unity for a WebXR-only project.
antipattern.reason: WebXR vs. native engine confusion is the #1 agentic mistake in spatial work. Three.js does not run on visionOS; Unity WebGL output is not the Unity XR runtime.

antipattern.description: Using "Reality Composer" and "Reality Composer Pro" interchangeably.
antipattern.reason: They are different applications with different capabilities. Reality Composer is the legacy iPad/iPhone tool; Reality Composer Pro is the macOS-only visionOS authoring environment.

antipattern.description: Generating performance estimates from agent output without device benchmarking.
antipattern.reason: LLM performance estimates for spatial assets are folklore. Triangle count, draw calls, and frame time must be measured on target hardware — emulators understate cost.
